touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Taylor Kelly introducing AskElephant's rep productivity solution. No response received.

Brief cold call with Taylor Kelly. Rep introduced AskElephant's automation capabilities for sales teams. Taylor expressed initial interest in learning more, mentioned recent transition to Head of RevOps role.

questions_asked.question: What specifically does your platform automate for sales reps?
questions_asked.answer: AskElephant automates CRM data entry, follow-up sequencing, and provides AI-powered insights to help reps focus on selling rather than admin work.

next_steps: Taylor to review calendar for qualification call

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn connection request sent with personalized note about RevOps challenges. No response.
Follow-up email sharing case study from similar professional services company. Taylor responded asking about implementation timeline and current customer base.

questions_asked.question: How long does typical implementation take?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are fully onboarded within 4-6 weeks, with initial value visible in the first two weeks.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Email confirming qualification call time and sharing discovery agenda. Taylor confirmed attendance and mentioned wanting to involve other stakeholders later.
next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for Tuesday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: Pre-call email with discovery questions to help Taylor prepare. Taylor acknowledged and shared that rep productivity is a top priority for Q2.
next_steps: Taylor to gather current metrics before call

touches.ai_summary: Email following qualification call with resources and ROI calculator. Taylor expressed interest in deeper discovery with use case examples.
next_steps: Schedule extended discovery call

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Taylor Kelly. Discussed current pain points: reps spending 40% of time on CRM admin, inconsistent data quality, manual follow-up tracking. Taylor new to role (3 months) and building case for RevOps investment. Budget authority rests with COO Carter Allen.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the focus on rep productivity right now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling the sales team from 45 to 75 reps this year, and our current processes won't scale. I need to prove RevOps can enable growth without proportional overhead.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Salesforce integration with bi-directional sync.

next_steps: Taylor to share current tech stack documentation
Rep to send discovery agenda for longer session

touches.ai_summary: Email with discovery session agenda and request for additional stakeholders. Taylor confirmed IT Director and Sales Manager should be involved for technical and end-user perspective.
next_steps: Taylor to invite Scarlett Nelson (IT) and Luke Agarwal (Sales)

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Taylor Kelly diving into workflows, current tools, and pain points. Discussed 3500+ employee scale, current manual CRM processes costing 15+ hours per rep per week. Taylor outlined use case: automation plus consulting for implementation. Mentioned need for executive buy-in from COO.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like 6 months after implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Reps spending less than 5 hours weekly on admin, data quality above 90%, and measurable increase in customer-facing time. I also need dashboards to prove ROI to Carter.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical price range for our company size?
questions_asked.answer: For your seat count and use case, typically $70-90K annually depending on modules and consulting hours.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in the decision?
questions_asked.answer: Carter Allen our COO has final budget approval. Scarlett in IT will need to vet security and integration. Luke manages the sales team and will be key for adoption.

next_steps: Rep to prepare technical overview for IT
Taylor to schedule demo with broader team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to clarify technical requirements and integration points. Taylor confirmed Salesforce is primary CRM, also using Outreach and Gong. Discussed data security requirements for IT review.

questions_asked.question: How does the consulting component work with the platform?
questions_asked.answer: We provide dedicated implementation consultants who customize workflows, train your team, and optimize configurations based on your specific processes.

next_steps: Rep to send security documentation to Scarlett
Schedule demo for following week

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Taylor walking through platform capabilities, API documentation, and integration architecture. Covered Salesforce sync, workflow automation builder, and analytics dashboard. Taylor asked detailed questions about customization limits and data residency.

questions_asked.question: Can we build custom automation workflows beyond your templates?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, the workflow builder supports custom logic, and our consulting team can help build complex automations specific to your processes.

questions_asked.question: Where is customer data stored?
questions_asked.answer: US-based AWS infrastructure with SOC2 Type II compliance. We can provide detailed data residency documentation for your IT team.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We're planning Q2 implementations, so ideally finalize by end of month to hit our growth targets.

next_steps: Send SOC2 report and integration specs to Scarlett
Schedule demo with Luke and end users

touches.ai_summary: First full platform demo with Taylor Kelly. Demonstrated CRM automation, email sequencing, and AI-powered insights dashboard. Walked through rep workflow from lead assignment through close. Taylor engaged, asked about reporting capabilities for exec visibility.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the executive dashboards?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, dashboards are fully customizable and we have templates for RevOps executives tracking productivity metrics.

questions_asked.question: Which capabilities are most critical for your Q2 goals?
questions_asked.answer: Automated CRM entry and follow-up workflows are priority one. Analytics are important but can phase in.

questions_asked.question: How does the AI handle our specific industry terminology?
questions_asked.answer: The AI learns from your historical data and can be trained on your terminology. Professional services customers typically see good accuracy within 2-3 weeks.

next_steps: Share executive dashboard examples
Schedule demo for Luke and sales team sample

touches.ai_summary: Demo focused on end-user experience for sales reps. Showed mobile app, Chrome extension, and daily workflow automation. Taylor brought account manager Yuki Hoffmann to provide feedback from user perspective. Yuki expressed enthusiasm about time savings.

questions_asked.question: How long does it take reps to learn the platform?
questions_asked.answer: Most reps are productive within first week. Our consulting team provides hands-on training and we have an onboarding certification program.

questions_asked.question: What would make reps actually adopt this versus current habits?
questions_asked.answer: The mobile app is key - if it's easier than manual CRM entry, they'll use it. Also manager visibility into activity helps drive accountability.

objections: Yuki mentioned concern about learning curve during busy season
next_steps: Send training timeline and change management guide
Schedule technical deep-dive with Scarlett

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sharing training resources and change management best practices. Taylor acknowledged and mentioned need to build business case for Carter Allen with ROI projections.

touches.ai_summary: Shorter demo focused on reporting and analytics for executive stakeholders. Walked through ROI tracking, productivity metrics, and forecasting capabilities. Taylor interested in proving value to COO.

questions_asked.question: Can we track ROI in real-time after implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, the analytics track time saved, data quality improvements, and rep productivity metrics with before/after comparisons.

next_steps: Prepare executive business case presentation
Schedule meeting with Carter Allen

touches.ai_summary: Email with draft proposal outline and pricing framework. Taylor requested to see formal proposal before bringing to Carter and procurement team including Luke and Scarlett.

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation with IT Director Scarlett Nelson joining. Deep dive on security protocols, SSO integration, API rate limits, and data encryption. Scarlett thorough but positive, asked about disaster recovery and SLA guarantees.

questions_asked.question: What's your uptime SLA and what happens if you miss it?
questions_asked.answer: 99.9% uptime SLA with service credits for any downtime. We publish status page and have 24/7 monitoring.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle data backup and recovery?
questions_asked.answer: Automated daily backups with point-in-time recovery. Customer data retained for 30 days in backup.

questions_asked.question: Any concerns from IT perspective that would block this?
questions_asked.answer: Need to review your disaster recovery documentation more carefully, but architecturally this looks solid. SSO integration is straightforward.

next_steps: Send disaster recovery runbook to Scarlett
Schedule security review follow-up if needed

touches.ai_summary: Email sharing formal proposal: $87,200 ACV for 59 seats with automations plus consulting package. Included implementation timeline, success metrics, and customer references. Taylor reviewing with team.
next_steps: Taylor to share proposal with Carter, Luke, and Scarlett
Schedule review meeting for following week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email answering Taylor's questions about contract terms, payment schedule, and consulting hour allocation. Taylor mentioned Carter wants to understand pricing breakdown before committing.

questions_asked.question: Can we split consulting hours across implementation and ongoing support?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we typically allocate 60% to implementation and 40% to ongoing optimization, but can adjust based on your needs.

next_steps: Schedule pricing review call
Prepare detailed cost breakdown

touches.ai_summary: Pricing call with Taylor Kelly to discuss proposal details. Taylor shared that budget is approved in principle but Carter wants to understand value justification. Luke raised concern about seat count - may not need full 59 initially. Discussed phased approach.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with fewer seats and expand?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can structure as 40 seats initially with expansion pricing locked in for additional seats in year one.

questions_asked.question: What's the concern with full seat count upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Luke wants to prove value with core team before rolling to everyone. Also managing Q2 budget across multiple initiatives.

objections: Pricing feels high for unproven ROI at our scale
next_steps: Rep to model phased pricing option
Schedule exec meeting with Carter Allen

touches.ai_summary: First exec meeting with COO Carter Allen and Taylor Kelly. Carter focused on business case, ROI timeline, and risk mitigation. Asked about customer references in professional services at similar scale. Interested but wants proof of value before full commitment.

questions_asked.question: What's realistic ROI timeline and how do we measure it?
questions_asked.answer: Customers typically see measurable productivity gains within 60 days. We track time saved per rep, data quality scores, and velocity metrics.

questions_asked.question: What if adoption fails - what's our out?
questions_asked.answer: Annual contract but we can include 90-day success milestone. If you're not seeing value, we can discuss off-ramp.

questions_asked.question: What would make you confident to move forward?
questions_asked.answer: Reference calls with similar companies, clear success metrics, and comfort with the investment level. Taylor's new to the role so I need to be confident in the partner.

objections: Investment is significant without proven ROI at our company
next_steps: Arrange reference calls with 2-3 professional services customers
Taylor to draft internal success criteria

touches.ai_summary: Second exec meeting with Carter Allen reviewing updated proposal with phased approach and success milestones. Carter appreciative of flexibility but raised questions about consulting value and whether platform can deliver without heavy services.

questions_asked.question: How much can we self-implement versus needing consulting?
questions_asked.answer: Platform is self-serve capable, but consulting accelerates time-to-value significantly. Most customers use consulting for custom workflows and change management.

questions_asked.question: Is the consulting component a concern from budget perspective?
questions_asked.answer: Somewhat. If we can reduce consulting hours and keep core platform capabilities, that helps the business case.

questions_asked.question: Can you share results from your reference customers?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, one professional services customer saved 12 hours per rep per week and improved data quality by 35% in first quarter.

next_steps: Rep to model reduced consulting package
Schedule reference calls

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Taylor Kelly after reference calls completed. Taylor shared positive feedback but Carter pushing for better pricing. Discussed reducing consulting hours from premium to standard package. Taylor transparent about competing priorities for Q2 budget.

questions_asked.question: What's your best pricing if we reduce consulting to standard package?
questions_asked.answer: We could adjust to $79,500 with standard consulting and 50 seats, maintaining expansion pricing for year one.

questions_asked.question: Is pricing the main blocker or are there other concerns?
questions_asked.answer: Pricing is main issue. Carter is supportive but watching Q2 budget closely. Need to make the numbers work.

objections: Budget is tight across multiple Q2 initiatives
next_steps: Rep to get approval for revised pricing

touches.ai_summary: Brief exec check-in with Carter Allen on revised proposal. Carter appreciates pricing movement but wants to finalize before month end. Confirmed Luke and Scarlett are aligned from their functional perspectives.

questions_asked.question: Can we lock this pricing for 30 days while we finalize internally?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, pricing valid through end of month. We can hold these terms.

next_steps: Carter to review final proposal with finance

touches.ai_summary: Final negotiation call with Taylor Kelly. Agreed on $87,200 ACV for 59 seats with automations plus consulting package. Taylor confirmed budget approved and ready to move to contracting. Discussed implementation timeline starting early next month to hit Q2 goals.

questions_asked.question: What's the approval process from here?
questions_asked.answer: Carter approved this morning. Now goes to legal for contract review, should be 5-7 days.

questions_asked.question: Can we start implementation planning before contract is fully signed?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can begin discovery and planning with your team while legal reviews. Formal kickoff once signed.
